workspace.chooseConnection: Choose a saved connection or create a new one to start querying

connection.deleteConfirm:
Delete connection "{{name}}"?

This will remove it from saved connections and close any active SSH tunnels.
connection.credentialsInfo: Credentials are sent directly to the Tauri backend and stored securely in your OS keychain.
connection.connectionStringDesc: Paste a PostgreSQL, MySQL, or SQLite connection string.
connection.name: Connection Name
connection.sshTunnelDesc: Tunnel the database connection through a bastion/jump host. Key-based auth is recommended. Password auth requires sshpass installed.
connection.advancedDesc: Connection pooling, TLS certificates, and custom parameters.
connection.connectionUriDesc: Paste a full connection URI with scheme (`postgresql://`, `mysql://`, or `sqlite:///`). Individual fields will be populated automatically.
connection.engineDesc: PostgreSQL is the safest default. MySQL and SQLite are marked experimental and may have limited support.
connection.advancedDesc2: Additional PostgreSQL connection parameters (libpq-compatible). Optional and only applied to PostgreSQL.
connection.noCustomParams: No custom parameters. Add one to pass extra PostgreSQL options.

table.tablePropertiesDesc: Configure nullability and UNIQUE constraints for columns.
table.tablePropertyEditingPostgresOnly: Table property editing is currently supported for PostgreSQL connections only.
table.noColumnsFound: No columns found for this table.

model.postgresOnly: Model workspace currently supports PostgreSQL only. Active engine: {{engine}}.
model.connectToUse: Connect to a database to use the model workspace.
model.createTableDesc: Define the new table on the diagram. This will be included in the migration preview and applied with the rest of your model changes.
model.ddlPostgresDesc: Statements execute in order inside one PostgreSQL transaction. On failure, the whole batch rolls back.
model.ddlEngineDesc: Statements execute in order for the active {{engine}} connection.
model.migrationChangesDesc: Review {{changes}} pending change(s) ({{statements}} SQL statement(s)) before applying.
model.noPendingChangesDesc: No pending changes. Add tables, columns, or relationships to the diagram first.
model.ruleDesc: PostgreSQL-only: queue RULE statements for apply.
model.triggerDesc: PostgreSQL-only: queue TRIGGER statements for apply.
model.rlsPolicyDesc: PostgreSQL-only: queue RLS statements (policy create/drop, enable/disable row level security).
model.catalogSummary: {{filtered}} of {{total}} tables in catalog; {{onDiagram}} on diagram
model.diagramHelp: Hover a table for connection handles. Drag nodes to move. Scroll to pan (Cmd+scroll to zoom). Space+drag pans. Shift+click multi-selects.
model.selectTableToInspect: Select a table to view its properties
model.headerColor: Color of this table's header on the diagram
model.subsetLoaded: Subset is loaded first for performance.
model.allTablesOnDiagram: All tables are currently on the diagram.
model.diagramEmptyHint: Open the Catalog tab and add tables. Use the Select tool to drag tables; Hand pans the canvas (Space + drag also pans).
model.loadAllConfirm: Load all {{count}} tables into the diagram? This may reduce responsiveness on large schemas.
model.selectTableToEditProperties: Select a table on the diagram or in the catalog to edit properties.

editor.selectTable: Select a table to enable row editing.
editor.runQuery: Run a query to edit rows.
editor.requiresPrimaryKey: Editing requires a primary key on the selected table.
editor.confirmWrite: This statement modifies data or schema. Run it anyway?
editor.deleteConfirm: Delete {{count}} selected row(s)? This action cannot be undone.
editor.queryHistoryDesc: Search and load previously executed queries.
editor.queryHistoryEmpty: Query history will appear here as you run queries.
editor.editingNotAvailableForExplain: Editing is not available for EXPLAIN output.
editor.outputTruncated: Output was truncated to keep the UI responsive.
editor.insertNoColumns: Run a query that returns at least one column (for example SELECT * FROM your_table LIMIT 1), then use Add row again.
editor.editingDisabled: Editing is disabled for this result set.
editor.noEditableChanges: No editable row changes found. Ensure rows have primary keys and edited values differ.
editor.noUsablePrimaryKey: None of the selected rows have a usable primary key.
editor.noVisibleColumnsToCopy: No visible data columns to copy.
editor.noVisibleColumnsToExport: No visible data columns to export.
editor.runQueryToSeeResults: Run a query to see results here.
editor.addRowHint: Add row is in the toolbar above. It needs a result grid with columns—run something like SELECT * FROM … LIMIT 50 first, then click Add row again.
editor.inlineAddRowHint: Inline add row needs a query that returns columns. Try a SELECT that lists the columns you want to fill.

veloxy.selectConnection: Select a connection before using Ask Veloxy.
veloxy.addApiKey: Add your OpenRouter API key in Settings → Veloxy.
veloxy.chooseModel: Choose a Veloxy model in Settings → Veloxy.
veloxy.generateFailed: Ask Veloxy failed to generate SQL.
veloxy.loadFailed: Failed to load Ask Veloxy conversation.
veloxy.clearFailed: Failed to clear Ask Veloxy conversation.
veloxy.needsConfirmation: Read query looks large/risky. Confirm before running.
veloxy.nonReadConfirmation: Non-read query requires confirmation before running.
veloxy.confirmationRequired: Confirmation required before running this statement.
veloxy.needsApiKey: Configure your OpenRouter API key in Settings to use Ask Veloxy.
veloxy.configureDesc: Add your OpenRouter API key and pick a model in Settings → Veloxy to enable the assistant.
veloxy.schemaAwareCopilotDesc: Ask about relationships, performance tradeoffs, or fixes. When you are ready, use Generate SQL from a reply — chat stays read-only until then.

settings.title: Settings
settings.themeDesc: Light, dark, or follow system preference.
settings.fontSizeDesc: Base font size across the application.
settings.monospaceFontDesc: Font family for the SQL editor.
settings.lineNumbersDesc: Show line numbers in the SQL editor.
settings.nullDisplayDesc: How NULL values appear in results.
settings.clickToCopyDesc: Single-click a cell to copy its value.
settings.autoReconnectDesc: Restore the last active connection on startup.
settings.healthPingDesc: How often to check connection health.
settings.providerDesc: Ask Veloxy uses OpenRouter with your own API key.
settings.openRouterApiKeyDesc: Used for Ask Veloxy model calls.
settings.modelDesc: Popular presets plus live model list from OpenRouter.
settings.errorToastsDesc: Show error toasts when operations fail.
settings.exportSettingsDesc: Download all settings as a JSON file.
settings.importSettingsDesc: Restore settings from a JSON file.
settings.clearHistoryDesc: Remove all saved query history and favorites.
settings.checkUpdatesDesc: See if a newer version is available on GitHub.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts.description: Available shortcuts across all panels. Press {{modKey}} + / to toggle.

onboarding.welcomeDesc: A fast, memory-efficient desktop client for PostgreSQL.
onboarding.welcomeTitle: SQL databases, unleashed on your desktop
onboarding.welcomeBody: Built for builders who live in SQL. VeloxDB keeps you in flow—snappy queries, crisp results, a workspace that feels premium. Your data stays between you and your database: private by design, secure by default, no extra cloud hop just to run a SELECT.
onboarding.enginesBody: Connect to the database you already run. PostgreSQL is the safest default for production workloads. MySQL and MariaDB work alongside it. SQLite is there for local files, prototypes, and edge cases—same editor, same sidebar, same shortcuts.
onboarding.enginesHighlight1: Saved connection profiles with SSL and SSH tunnels
onboarding.enginesHighlight2: Switch databases from the sidebar without reconnecting rituals
onboarding.enginesHighlight3: Engine-aware SQL linting and result grids
onboarding.queryTitle: A real editor for serious SQL
onboarding.queryBody: Multi-tab Monaco editing, schema-aware autocomplete, streaming results for heavy reads, and EXPLAIN when milliseconds matter. Export to CSV or JSON, edit rows inline, and keep history per connection.
onboarding.queryHighlight2: Virtualized grids for large result sets
onboarding.modelTitle: See your schema as a diagram
onboarding.modelBody: Toggle to Model for a live ER canvas—tables, columns, and relationships laid out clearly. Drag objects, inspect indexes and triggers, preview DDL before you apply, and export the diagram when you need to share context.
onboarding.veloxyBody: Veloxy reads your connected database—tables, columns, and foreign keys—and helps you explore, explain, and generate SQL in plain language. Chat to understand relationships, then generate and run read queries with guardrails when you are ready.
onboarding.veloxyHighlight1: Natural-language chat grounded in your live schema
onboarding.veloxyHighlight2: Generate SQL from a reply, with confirmation for writes
onboarding.veloxyHighlight3: Configure your model in Settings → Veloxy
onboarding.connectionsBody: Save trusted profiles and jump between databases in one click—no ritual re-entering of host and port every session. From PostgreSQL and MySQL to local SQLite files, we keep the path short so you wire fast and ship faster.
onboarding.workspacesBody: A real editor for serious SQL, streaming results when you need answers now, and EXPLAIN when milliseconds matter. Toggle to Model for a live map of tables and relationships—clarity over chaos, all in one app.
onboarding.shortcutsTitle: Shortcuts that keep you in the zone
onboarding.shortcutsBody: Keyboard-driven workflow: Cmd/Ctrl+P for the palette, Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+C for connections, Cmd/Ctrl+Enter to run. Less mouse mileage, fewer tab dances—stay locked in on what matters.
onboarding.createConnectionDesc: Connect to a PostgreSQL, MySQL, or SQLite database.
onboarding.readyToQueryDesc: You're all set! Start writing SQL queries.
onboarding.exploreSchema: Explore your database schema in the sidebar
onboarding.useModelWorkspace: Use the Model workspace to visualize your schema
